governing_requirement: Freeze and execute full-frame/crop/resize/pad/flip/project transforms with typed spaces/dimensions, interpolation, padding, rounding, orientation, side-swap, canonical hashes, inverse and measured round-trip evidence; reject opaque parameters, bounds/dimension/order/inverse/tolerance and protected-region drift.

dependency_state.disposition: MF-P6-09.02 remains dependency-blocked; this revalidation awards only current structural foundation credit.

prior_evidence.disposition: Accepted for its requirement mapping, missing-proof inventory and additive implementation packet. Its obsolete queued 09.01 prerequisite description is superseded by bridge_identity_gap_revalidation_20260719.json.

verified_foundations: The frozen request contract declares source/crop/output spaces, typed crop/resize/pad/horizontal-flip/project parameters, dimensions, interpolation/padding/rounding/orientation/side-swap facts, zero-based ordered steps, per-step and chain hashes, inverse strategy and round-trip policy.

Semantic validation recomputes step/chain hashes, requires contiguous sequence and dimension/space continuity, enforces operation-specific parameter forms, crop bounds, resize/pad/flip dimensions, finite invertible project matrices and source/output binding.

Target/protected input records bind the exact transform chain, source decoded pixels, owner, coordinates, dimensions, artifact identity and authority; receipts/certificates bind executed-step and round-trip observations.
The frozen semantic profile requires executable transform replay, and legacy crop affine schema/tests provide a reusable closed crop representation.
Six focused bridge/semantic/crop tests pass and Ruff passes; all governing source hashes match the accepted prior audit.

direct_probes.mutation: Replace the typed matrix/clip/rounding parameters with one opaque digest and recompute trusted hashes/signature.
direct_probes.interpretation: The frozen schema correctly rejects opaque-only transform parameters.

direct_probes.mutation: Use a singular 3x3 project matrix and recompute step/chain/artifact/request hashes.
direct_probes.interpretation: Structural validation correctly rejects a noninvertible projection.

direct_probes.mutation: Set maximum_error_px to 1000000.0 and recompute every dependent hash/signature.
direct_probes.gap: No pinned producer policy caps tolerance or measures actual forward/inverse error; any nonnegative asserted tolerance is accepted.
Validation describes geometry but never executes points, boxes, masks, protected regions or ontology side swaps.

confirmed_gaps: No producer transform executor applies the declared chain to points, boxes, strict-binary masks and protected regions in exact order.
No inverse builder executes crop/resize/pad/flip/project reversal under one self-hashed rounding/subpixel/tolerance policy.
No policy bounds maximum round-trip tolerance; the request may self-assert an arbitrarily large value.
No runtime measures round-trip error or binds actual input/output raster hashes, executed steps, inverse chain and result into strict evidence.
No ontology-aware horizontal-flip implementation swaps left/right labels and geometry exactly once.
No exhaustive vectors cover every operation, order/dimension/bounds/interpolation/padding/rounding/orientation/inverse/side-swap/protected-region failure and order invariance.
No downstream consumer can distinguish structurally valid declared transforms from transforms actually executed against the bound pixels.

implementation_packet.path_state: All four allowed paths remain absent, untracked and clean; they are disjoint from the preserved 09.01 additive identity scope and dirty 08.05 scope.
implementation_packet.next_action: Hold until MF-P6-09.01 is implemented/adopted through a qualified exact-project path; do not dispatch or duplicate while worker lanes remain disabled.

claim_boundary: This audit credits the current typed/hash-bound structural transform contract and validators only. It does not execute or invert geometry, measure round-trip error, prove ontology side swaps, bind runtime replay evidence, satisfy MF-P6-09.01, or complete MF-P6-09.02.
